---
title: "OrgTree — Identity Modernization"
subtitle: "x.500 AD Hierarchy to Flat Entra ID — The Complete A-Z Suite"
date: 2026-04-19
---

::: {.callout-warning}
## Aspirational — canonically declared, not yet fully adopted

The OrgTree specifications are canonical and govern all identity modernization work.
Operational instantiation across production tenants is under development.
:::

## The Problem Nobody Talks About

When Microsoft deprecated Active Directory's hierarchical model in favor of Entra ID's
flat directory, they solved the *identity* problem. But they broke the *governance* problem.

Active Directory's Organizational Unit tree was never just a folder structure. It was:

- **A delegation model** — OU-level permissions defined who could manage what
- **A policy inheritance engine** — Group Policy Objects cascaded through the tree
- **An operational boundary** — each OU represented a unit of accountability
- **A security scope** — group membership, resource access, and trust relationships traced back to OU placement

Entra ID has none of this. It gives you a flat directory with powerful tools — dynamic
groups, Administrative Units, Conditional Access policies — but no framework for how to
use them to replace what the OU tree was doing.

The OrgTree canon is that framework.

## The OrgPath Architecture

The core innovation is the **OrgPath** — a deterministic, attribute-encoded organizational
hierarchy that reconstructs AD's governance model inside Entra ID's flat directory. Instead
of OU placement, governance relationships are encoded in user extension attributes using a
structured codebook.

**Example:** An employee in the Finance department, Budget division, East region would carry:

`extensionAttribute1: FIN-BUD-EAST`

This single attribute drives:

- **Dynamic group membership** — automatically placed in `SG-FIN-BUD-EAST-Users`
- **Administrative Unit scoping** — managed by the `AU-FIN-BUD` delegation boundary
- **Conditional Access targeting** — policies applied via group membership, not manual assignment
- **License assignment** — governed by OrgPath-to-SKU mapping rules
- **Drift detection** — any mismatch between HR source and OrgPath value is flagged

## The Complete A-Z Suite

Twenty-eight canonical artifacts organized into four governance layers:

### Identity Layer — What Gets Migrated

| ID | Title | Purpose |
|----|-------|---------|
| UIAO_150 | Executive Summary | Architecture overview, governance principles, migration strategy |
| UIAO_151 | OrgPath Codebook | The master encoding scheme — every valid OrgPath value, hierarchy rules, and validation regex |
| UIAO_152 | Dynamic Group Library | Complete library of dynamic membership rules keyed to OrgPath segments |
| UIAO_153 | Attribute Mapping Table | Full x.500 DN to flat-attribute translation — every AD attribute to its Entra ID equivalent |

### Structure Layer — How It Is Organized

| ID | Title | Purpose |
|----|-------|---------|
| UIAO_154 | Delegation Matrix (AUs and Roles) | Administrative Unit design — who manages what, RBAC role assignments, scope boundaries |
| UIAO_155 | Governance Workflow Catalog | Every workflow type: onboarding, offboarding, transfer, role change, exception handling |
| UIAO_156 | Migration Runbook (OU to Entra) | Step-by-step migration execution — phased rollout, validation gates, rollback triggers |
| UIAO_157 | Diagram Pack (Text-Rendered) | PlantUML architecture diagrams — OrgPath flow, group hierarchy, delegation boundaries |
| UIAO_158 | OrgPath JSON Schema | Machine-readable schema for OrgPath validation — used by drift engine and CI pipelines |

### Policy Layer — What Governs Behavior

| ID | Title | Purpose |
|----|-------|---------|
| UIAO_159 | PowerShell Validation Module | Validation cmdlets — Test-OrgPath, Test-DynamicGroupRule, Test-AUScope |
| UIAO_160 | Governance Enforcement Test Suite | Pester test suite — validates every governance rule against live or mock tenant state |
| UIAO_161 | Enforcement Decision Trees | If-then decision logic for every governance scenario — no ambiguity, no judgment calls |
| UIAO_162 | SLA Heatmap and Owner Reliability Model | Tracks governance artifact owners, response times, reliability scores, escalation triggers |
| UIAO_164 | Execution Substrate Integration Layer | Interface spec for Copilot-to-Execution Substrate handoff — what gets governed vs. executed |
| UIAO_165 | Enforcement Test Harness (Mock Tenant) | Mock tenant configuration for testing governance rules without touching production |

### Governance Layer — What Watches Everything

| ID | Title | Purpose |
|----|-------|---------|
| UIAO_163 | Drift Detection Engine Specification | The core drift engine — schema drift, value drift, hierarchy drift, orphan detection, phantom detection |
| UIAO_166 | Governance Boundary Impact Model | Maps every governance decision to its blast radius — what breaks if this changes |
| UIAO_167 | SLA Escalation Playbooks | Time-bound escalation procedures when governance violations exceed SLA thresholds |
| UIAO_168 | Canonical Repository Structure | Defines the repo layout — where every artifact lives, naming conventions, CI integration |
| UIAO_169 | Governance OS State Machine | Formal state machine — every governance object has defined states and legal transitions |
| UIAO_170 | Identity Risk Scoring Model | Risk scores for identity objects based on privilege level, staleness, drift history |
| UIAO_171 | Multi-Cloud Boundary Model (GCC-Moderate Safe) | Defines what is in-scope and out-of-scope for M365 GCC-Moderate governance |
| UIAO_172 | Canonical Contributor Workflow (PR to Merge) | How changes enter the canon — PR, validation, review, merge, provenance chain |
| UIAO_173 | Canonical Error Taxonomy | Every error type the governance engine can produce — codes, severity, remediation |
| UIAO_174 | Governance Telemetry Model | What gets measured, how it gets reported, dashboard specifications |
| UIAO_175 | Identity Graph Normalization Model | How identity relationships are normalized across AD, Entra ID, HR systems, and apps |
| UIAO_176 | Full Governance OS Glossary | Authoritative definitions for every term used across the modernization canon |

## Five Drift Categories

The drift detection engine (UIAO_163) classifies every detected anomaly into exactly
one of five categories:

| Category | Definition | Example |
|----------|-----------|---------|
| **Schema Drift** | The structure of a governance artifact has changed | A dynamic group rule references an attribute not in the OrgPath schema |
| **Value Drift** | An attribute value does not match the canonical source | User's OrgPath says FIN-BUD-EAST but HR system says FIN-TAX-EAST |
| **Hierarchy Drift** | Parent-child governance relationships are broken | An AU references an OrgPath segment that no longer exists in the codebook |
| **Orphan Drift** | An object exists in the tenant but has no canonical governance anchor | A security group exists that is not in the Dynamic Group Library |
| **Phantom Drift** | A canonical artifact references an object that does not exist in the tenant | The codebook defines an OrgPath segment with zero matching users |

## Source Files

All OrgTree narrative canon now lives at [`src/uiao/canon/UIAO_150…UIAO_176`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/tree/main/src/uiao/canon) (renamed from the retired `MOD_xxx` namespace per [ADR-060](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-060-mod-namespace-flatten-into-uiao-canon.md)).
Executable companion modules are at [`src/uiao/modernization/orgtree/`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/tree/main/src/uiao/modernization/orgtree).
Registry metadata is in [`src/uiao/canon/document-registry.yaml`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/document-registry.yaml) (the orgtree-side sibling registry was deleted by ADR-060).
